methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
T1 Energy is a public-stage climate and energy company that has raised over $1 billion to date. The valuation universe for this company is sparse: the Climate & Energy sector contains 19 peer companies, but the peer-median approach is inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables. A same-stage peer set yields only 2 companies, while adjacent-stage and similar-tier filters produce zero datapoints. This falls well short of the three-company minimum required for robust peer triangulation.

The comp universe consists of 1 public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. The extreme sparsity of public comparables and the heterogeneity of private capital structures across battery technology, carbon capture, and advanced energy subsectors limit the applicability of conventional multiples-based valuation methods.

Without sufficient same-stage or tier-matched comps, traditional peer-median and EV/Revenue approaches cannot be reliably deployed. A defensible valuation would require alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis tied to specific production milestones, sum-of-the-parts modeling across discrete business units, or precedent transaction analysis—none of which are available in the current dataset. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of a sufficiently dense peer set to anchor relative valuation, a constraint inherent to the frontier-tech landscape in climate and energy.
